title: Jody E. Aldcorn
content:
People.ContactEmail jaldcorn@mccarthy.ca
- British Columbia, 1998
- University of British Columbia
- Osgoode Hall Law School
With extensive industry insight and knowledge, clients get solutions to their most complex corporate and commercial law matters.
Jody is a trusted advisor to clients navigating complex corporate and commercial law matters, with a particular focus on infrastructure development, project finance, and aviation. As a partner in the Vancouver office, Jody is recognized for her strategic insight and holistic approach, helping clients achieve their business objectives efficiently and effectively.
Jody has significant experience in infrastructure projects in Canada, the United States and the Caribbean, and provides advice throughout the full life cycle of a project, including in connection with project procurement, structuring, bidding, transactional matters and ongoing governance, management and operational issues. Clients value Jody's outstanding track record and her commitment to their business, which allows her to tailor her approach to best target all aspects of their projects.
Before joining McCarthy Tétrault, Jody served as Director of Legal Services for the Vancouver Airport Authority, overseeing all aspects of legal operations, including construction, engineering, commercial development, and finance. Jody has also acted as lead counsel and corporate secretary to one of the world's premier international airport developers and operators. She regularly draws upon this experience to offer clients a broader perspective while delivering value and seamless coverage.
Jody is an active member of the aviation community and a sought-after speaker at industry conferences, including, most recently, Global Airport Development Conferences held in Germany, Grand Cayman and Portugal, where she has spoken about public-private partnership models for airports and the potential privatization of Canada's Airports.
Jody holds an LLB from the University of British Columbia and an LLM in Tax Law from Osgoode Hall Law School, and is admitted to the bar of British Columbia.  She completed the Canadian Securities Course, CICA In-Depth Tax Course (Levels I- III), and, most recently, the Directors Education Program, Institute of Corporate Directors, Toronto Rotman School of Management, ICD.D.

#### Recent transactions include:
- advising Vantage Airport Group Ltd. in connection with the acquisition of FSM Management Group Inc. and AvEnergy Management Services Inc.;
- advising TradePort International Corporation in connection with the execution of a CAD$400 million 49-year agreement to manage the John C. Munro Hamilton Airport;
- advising Vantage Group and Corsair Infrastructure Partners, as lead developer and equity holder respectively, in connection with the US$4.2B John F. Kennedy International Airport Terminal 6 Redevelopment Project;
- advising Aecon Concessions in connection with the L.F. Wade International Airport Redevelopment Project, a 30-year concession granted by the Government of Bermuda to finance, construct and operate a new airport terminal and the subsequent sale of a minority interest in the project company
- advising a member of the successful proponent in connection with the US$5.1 billion LaGuardia Airport Central Terminal Building Replacement Project and in connection with certain subsequent secondary market transactions
- advising the successful proponent in connection with the development of a food & beverage and retail concession program at Kansas City International Airport;
- advising the successful proponent in connection with the redevelopment and operation of concession space at the William H. Gray III 30th Street Station Amtrak Station in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania;
- advising a Tier 1 Canadian airport authority in connection with various financial matters, including issuances of floating rate notes and debentures with a collective value of CAD $1 billion;
- advising an international infrastructure client on a US$300 million bilateral syndicated credit facility;
- advising Canadian airport operators in connection with various strategic and operational matters, including in relation to aviation policy, land use, fuel supply issues, airline relationships and contractual disputes.
